what_it_does: Defines the EXPAND_CRITERION role and its Pydantic output schema for turning one candidate criteria-group into a full two-faced loop-prep completion criterion as part of a spec-compiler MAP step.
observations: The file is a pure role definition: a long docstring, a Pydantic model, and a ROLE dict.
ExpandCriterionOut enforces six fields: id, function, form, files_touched, reuse_or_netnew, and preserves.
FORM is mandatory; backend-only groups with no operator-facing surface must use the literal "n/a".
reuse_or_netnew must be honest: "reuse:<file>" only when confident, otherwise "net-new".
input_addresses is ("utterance",) only; group_id, what, and systems_touched ride inside the unit dict.
model_binding requires chat and json capabilities and sets default_model to None for the resident swarm.
mode_scope is {"spec"} to mark a spec-compiler context without touching the listening cast.
The prompt_template instructs JSON-only output and gives two contrasting examples.
standouts: The two-faced criterion requirement: FUNCTION verifiable by use plus FORM where a surface exists.
The no-fiction reuse-claim rule, with explicit downstream grounding by ground_criterion.
The axis-inversion design: one role is fanned by run_items over N group units via the utterance input.
The advisory/floor framing: it drafts criteria and never builds or auto-approves.
conventions: Follows the roles/* pattern of pairing a Pydantic output schema with a ROLE dict.
Uses mode_scope {"spec"} as a cast-beyond-listening seam.
Defaults model to None to keep the resident 4B swarm floor.
Keeps declared inputs minimal to avoid dead declared-extras on a run_items-fanned MAP.
Heavy inline commentary explains design rationale and invariants.
concerns: Prompt examples are hard-coded and may drift from actual repo paths or design-system details.
files_touched is best-effort from the LLM with no in-file validation against the repo.
The large docstring/prompt mix makes the file harder to scan quickly.
No runtime validation logic beyond the Pydantic schema; no-fiction reuse enforcement depends on a downstream role.
notes: A spec-compiler MAP role that drafts a two-faced completion criterion for one decomposed group, with strict honesty rules on reuse and form.
questions: What exact validation does complete() apply against the schema (referenced but not shown)?
How is the per-unit corpus-slice or few-shot injected into the unit dict in practice?
What is the resident "4B swarm" and how is default_model None resolved at runtime?
How does ground_criterion confirm or override the preliminary reuse_or_netnew value?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it defines a single-group criterion expansion role with the declared output schema and advisory constraints.
apparent_themes: Axis inversion: one role fanned over many units
intention_signals: The file is trying to encode a repeatable, auditable step that converts fuzzy feature groups into concrete, verifiable loop-prep criteria while preventing common failures: function-only specs, fabricated reuse claims, and premature building.
novelty: The explicit two-faced FUNCTION/FORM split and the preliminary-but-honest reuse_or_netnew field with downstream grounding are distinctive design choices for a criterion schema.
contribution: Provides a structured output schema and role prompt that enforce function+form completeness and honest reuse classification for one unit of a spec-compiler MAP stage.
summary_for_embedding: Defines the EXPAND_CRITERION role and ExpandCriterionOut Pydantic schema for converting one candidate criteria-group into a two-faced loop-prep completion criterion with function, form, files_touched, reuse_or_netnew, and preserves fields.
intention_for_embedding: Intended as the MAP step of a spec-compiler that drafts auditable completion criteria for each decomposed group before downstream grounding and triad synthesis.

symbols.does: Pydantic output schema for a single expanded two-faced criterion.
symbols.description: Defines the six-field contract (id, function, form, files_touched, reuse_or_netnew, preserves) that the expand_criterion role emits. It enforces that every criterion has both a behaviour face and a form face, an honest reuse claim, and a no-regression preservation statement.
